description: postinstall script runs `npx playwright install chromium --with-deps` automatically on npm install; --with-deps triggers OS-level system package installation and may invoke sudo on Linux
suggested_fix: Move Playwright install to an explicit setup script and document it in README; remove the postinstall hook or gate it behind an env var opt-in

description: $ARGUMENTS interpolated unquoted into `npx designlang battle $ARGUMENTS`; shell metacharacters in user input (semicolon, pipe, backtick) allow arbitrary command execution
suggested_fix: Validate that $ARGUMENTS matches expected URL/flag pattern before passing to shell, or instruct Claude to quote the expansion: `npx designlang battle "$ARGUMENTS"`

description: $ARGUMENTS interpolated unquoted into `npx designlang brand $ARGUMENTS`; shell metacharacters in user input allow arbitrary command execution
suggested_fix: Validate $ARGUMENTS or quote the expansion before passing to the shell

description: git clone from hardcoded external GitHub fork URL at developer setup time; if the fork is compromised, arbitrary code is pulled and installed
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific commit SHA rather than a mutable branch name; add hash verification step

description: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk dependency uses caret range ^1.29.0 allowing automatic minor/patch upgrades without explicit review
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version in package.json and commit package-lock.json; use `npm ci` in CI

description: chalk dependency uses caret range ^5.3.0 allowing automatic minor/patch upgrades without explicit review
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version in package.json and commit package-lock.json

description: commander dependency uses caret range ^12.0.0 allowing automatic minor/patch upgrades without explicit review

description: playwright dependency uses caret range ^1.42.0 allowing automatic minor/patch upgrades without explicit review
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version in package.json and commit package-lock.json

description: `npm install --no-audit` suppresses npm's built-in security advisory check, hiding known vulnerable transitive dependencies
suggested_fix: Remove --no-audit flag; resolve any advisories that surface

description: Command declares no allowed-tools frontmatter field; any tool may be invoked without declared scope
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` to frontmatter

description: Command declares no allowed-tools frontmatter field; any tool may be invoked without declared scope
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` to frontmatter

description: Command declares no allowed-tools frontmatter field; any tool may be invoked without declared scope
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` to frontmatter

description: Post-run flow has at least four distinct steps (run → read markdown → present summary → offer follow-ups) described in prose with bullet lists rather than numbered steps
suggested_fix: Replace prose + bullets with a numbered list: 1. Run extraction 2. Read *-design-language.md 3. Present summary 4. Offer follow-ups

description: "tight summary" — "tight" is a vague descriptor that does not specify length, format, or content requirements
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete specification, e.g. "Present a 6-bullet summary covering palette, type, spacing, WCAG score, components, and notable signals"

description: Command declares no allowed-tools frontmatter field; any tool may be invoked without declared scope
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` to frontmatter

description: Command declares no allowed-tools frontmatter field; any tool may be invoked without declared scope
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` to frontmatter

description: Command declares no allowed-tools frontmatter field; any tool may be invoked without declared scope
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` to frontmatter

description: "most distinctive crossover" — "most distinctive" is subjective and gives no concrete selection criterion
suggested_fix: Define a concrete heuristic, e.g. "the axis whose contributing site's token values differ most from the other site's corresponding tokens"

description: Command declares no allowed-tools frontmatter field; any tool may be invoked without declared scope
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` to frontmatter

description: Post-run flow (read index → tell user which file to open → offer --open) is described in a single prose sentence without numbered steps
suggested_fix: Add numbered post-run steps: 1. Read comparison index file 2. Tell user which HTML file to open 3. Offer to auto-open with --open

description: Command declares no allowed-tools frontmatter field; any tool may be invoked without declared scope
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` to frontmatter

description: "key findings" — "key" is a vague quantifier; does not specify which findings are mandatory or how to rank them
suggested_fix: Enumerate the required findings explicitly (e.g. primary palette, type families, spacing base, WCAG score, component patterns, notable signals)

description: "notable design decisions" — "notable" is a vague quantifier giving no selection criterion
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete list: "shadows (y-offset, blur, color), border radii, animation durations"

description: "relevant guide" in included AGENTS.md — "relevant" is a vague quantifier that does not indicate which guide to read for which task
suggested_fix: Name the specific guides or provide a lookup strategy, e.g. "Read node_modules/next/dist/docs/routing.md for routing, node_modules/next/dist/docs/rendering.md for RSC"
